payload.title: Connor Christou used AI to fight cancer
payload.fact: Connor Christou fed blood results, scan data, wearable output, and journal entries into Claude to fight cancer.
supporting_evidence: Connor Christou fed everything tied tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude

payload.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models
payload.fact: Asian AI startups launched new models with Mythos-like capabilities.
supporting_evidence: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban

payload.title: Anthropic Mythos authorized for use by US companies and agencies
payload.fact: More than 100 US companies and government agencies are authorized to use Anthropic Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.
supporting_evidence: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees

payload.title: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request
payload.fact: OpenAI limited the rollout of GPT-5.6 following a government request.
supporting_evidence: OpenAI limits GPT-5.6 rollout after government request

payload.title: OpenAI hires Uber India chief
payload.fact: OpenAI hired the Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US.
supporting_evidence: OpenAI poaches Uber India chief to lead its biggest market outside the US
The hire marks OpenAI's latest push into India, expanding offices, partnerships and hiring

payload.title: OpenAI develops Jalapeño custom inference chip
payload.fact: OpenAI developed Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom.
supporting_evidence: OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom
Joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of single-supplier risk

payload.title: Apple Vision Pro exec Paul Meade leaving for OpenAI
payload.fact: Paul Meade, Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is leaving Apple to join OpenAI’s hardware team.
supporting_evidence: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team
